reviews.review_text: I’d easily recommend Baldur’s Gate 3 to anyone who likes RPGs, fantasy stories, or games where your choices actually matter. It’s one of those games that completely pulls you in after a few hours because every decision feels important, whether it changes relationships, quests, or even entire outcomes later in the story. Nothing really feels “scripted” the way a lot of games do. The characters are probably the best part for me. Everyone in your party feels like a real person with their own problems, personalities, and motives instead of just side characters following you around. I got attached to the companions way more than I expected to, and the dialogue actually made me care about what happened to them. The combat can seem overwhelming at first, especially if you’re not used to turn-based games, but once it clicks it becomes really satisfying. There are so many different ways to approach fights that it never feels repetitive. You can play smart, chaotic, stealthy, or just completely improvise and somehow it still works. Another thing I liked is how much freedom the game gives you. Most games pretend to give choices, but Baldur’s Gate 3 actually lets you solve situations however you want. Sometimes the funniest moments happen when your original plan completely fails and the game just rolls with it instead of punishing you for trying something different. Overall, it’s one of the few games that actually feels worth the time because there’s so much detail, replay value, and effort put into it. Even after finishing it, I still wanted to go back and see how different choices would change everything. 10/10!

At first I was not sure I wanted to buy it
I was afraid that I would not like it when I played it
But
This game became the best game of my life
I loved all the features of the game from the dialogues to the characterization to ...

Anyway, the only negative thing I can say about it is the photo mode of the game, which I expected much more and better from it

If you recently bought the game
Have fun

A game in which a random book/rune you read at your first hours can change the outcome of a conversation you are making near your last hours. Splendid, a must play if you are interested in RPG!

Also you can pet animals which is enough just by itself

I have too many hours in this game. This has been my favioute game for several years now. I even finished honour mode. It is high time I left a review.

Absolute cinema - everything out of 10. Perfection.

I highly recommend this game to anyone that cares for a rich story with complex worldbuilding and great turn-based combat. Your choices ACTUALLY matter, which is honestly pretty rare for RPGs, making the game very re-playable. Each party member has their own life and ambitions before coming into your camp which adds to the depth of the story as a whole. No two characters are identical, though all of their pasts seem to be united by one thing: [spoiler]a struggle for freedom[/spoiler]. I personally enjoyed witnessing the ethical decisions that had positive and negative consequences on both sides, showing that no choice is necessarily always the right one.

I am by no means a D&D player, nor am I well versed in the universe, though BG3 does a good way at holding your hand and teaching you the mechanics while also letting you discover some features for yourself. You get to learn about the races, classes, and monsters (and more) as they are all integrated within the game and there's always a book or NPC where you can learn more about the vast world that is Faerûn.

As for the gameplay, the combat is incredibly satisfying and enjoyable without feeling excruciatingly difficult as some strategy games do. However, I'd recommend playing on the Explorer (easy) difficulty if you're not that proficient with strategy games, but the Balanced (normal) difficulty is pretty fair. There are so many possible loadouts that it's almost overwhelming (in a good way). My suggestion is to not get too bogged down on the details the first time around. If you want to min-max everything, go for it! If not, you can still make it through to the end.

Lastly, I wanted to mention the soundtrack. The instrumental tracks are relaxing when you're exploring and intense when you're fighting for your life, just as they should be. I've listened to some outside of the game, like [spoiler]"Last Light"[/spoiler] (Act 2) which somehow makes me feel nostalgic for a game I started just over a year ago. The vocal tracks are also amazing, [spoiler]"Raphael's Final Act" makes the battle against Raphael feel as epic as it sounds[/spoiler] (Act 3).

Thank you for reading this. Please play this game. You won't regret it.

Play as paladin.
Choose [Lie] in dialog.
Game crashes.
Immersive oathbreaking experience.

I can only recommend this game if you truly hate yourself.

For what it's worth I really enjoyed the game mostly, as my hours should give away. The interactions, characters and all (even if somewhat a little corny or stiff sometimes), are crafted well. 
But you have to be a masochist to finish it, as you crawl back after every crash, getting more lashes, and begging the game engine to survive a single playthrough, because this game will raw dog you with corrupted files over and over and over. 
In my 54 hour playthrough, I've had the pleasure of receiving 3 black screens as my files repeatedly got corrupted. But why stop there, it also crashed my computer twice out of generosity, specifically on act III. The developers must have smoked one to come up with a spaghetti engine.

Starting this game up is like russian roulette, your game will run for countless sessions but it's inevitable that it will give up and die on you, taking your game files and hours of save files to the void. Not to mention if your session does work, you have the delightful chance of experiencing the game as a deaf person; having all your game audio absent, or as a confused demented person; having quest markers pointing you off the cliff or to a phantom character outside unreachable boundaries of the world itself, making numerous quests tedious.
I am truly convinced this game allows for multiple ways to interact with quests, or skip them, is because one way or another, a part of the quest will certainly break.

Before you even consider dropping 60,- (or whatever your currency is) for this meal filled with broken glass shards, these issues are not exclusive to me, but for countless of consumers who bought this between 2023 and the present. So if you can accept your fate before starting your playthrough, that Krampus will snatch your files from you unannounced, at any given moment in time, then you'll be alright.

Amazing game held by duct tape.

Great game. Played it with three friends. One of us got insta-killed by a trap, the other captured and thrown in jail, and our balding warlock hexed our final party member. All of this happened in the first hour of the game.

One of the best stories i've ever had the honor of playing, not to go into spoilers, i've finished the game with the "best" ending and it just left me empty and craving for more, but also happy knowing i've accomplished this grand adventure. Thank you Larian for a great game that will be etched in my memory for the years to come.
The game can be fun but it's so buggy and frustrating just play it's hard to recommend.

The game has a lot to offer, but in it's current state, 2 years after release, if you want to roleplay as anything but what the intended path is: don't bother.

For multiple hours I've been trying to tolerate many sequence breaks, buggy triggers and wonky fights, but the breaking point is how the game punished me for having the one race that doesn't let me gain an inspiration event... let me explain:

Spoilers for Act 1:
[spoiler]So I'm Dark Urge as a Female Drow, I'm trying to roleplay as an evil character. I'm on the "goblin temple" where Gut is. So I go to the torture chamber to get a Dark Urge inspiration. The goblin tortures see me and I say to step aside and let me handle it. They obey and decide to leave the place... but I have no option to torture the prisoner. I can talk to the prisoner and get information about Halsin and the Nightsong, I can pick up the torture weapons, but I can't attack the prisoner or have a dialogue option that lets me torture him. No option as a drow allows me to torture the prisoner. So the work-around is to use Disguise Self, turn to another race and THEN the option will appear for me to torture... why? Why is this even a thing, considering Drows are basically the more intended-to-be evil aligned races in the entire game and THEY are the ones that miss out on one of the most evil actions on act 1? It's stupid on several ways, and this is a drow-exclusive interaction Larian Studios knowingly programmed without the option to torture.

And if we must point out other issues I have had so far with my playthroughs of this game: NPCs don't react to the bodies of their own kin on ground.... so basically I wanted to slaughter all the druids in the emerald grove, so I, one-by-one, sometimes two at a time, used Performance and Minor Illusion to guide them to some outside area, and repeatedly killed them outside of sight of other druids. And that way I was able to kill all druids with none of them suspecting a thing or reacting to the dead druid bodies.

I accepted to be on a romance with Lae'zel, but with no sex scene yet, it was just the flag to trigger the initial romance. When I went outside of camp, Shadowheart and Laezel were talking about how good we were on bed, even though we never did anything.

Another major issue: at night, Astarion confided to me about his vampiric nature, and I asked about Cazador and his backstory, so on... then, on the same night, the cutscene where Astarion tries to bite me plays out, and my character (and the game) act like this was a huge revelation and they did not know about it. The next morning, the same dialogue options about Cazador and his backstory were there as if I had not triggered them already, so the game got confused with flags.

It also happened that goblins have shot through walls with their arrows to hit my characters in some combat during the blighted village. And it wasn't through a window or crack, it was literal solid wood wall, a goblin was on the other side and Astarion on the other, they shot and hit me, and Astarion couldn't hit back with his bow.

I've also faced many wonky path-finding moments where my characters went through extremely long paths for something that was mere meters away from them, happened at least twice: in the goblin camp and in the dark cave below blighted village.

And there's no search option on the adventurer's chest, which can easily have over 100 items in just a few hours of exploring (which the game heavily encourages)

The game continuously makes several time-restricting scenarios/ideas in the earlygame, which would make you think you shouldn't long rest as that could mean events will happen while you sleep, but the truth is that next to nothing happens if you long rest, in fact you HAVE to if you want to progress character stories. My issue with the long rest system is that it is inversely proportional to the story's narrative and what you would think is going to happen or should happen, and the paranoia that events MIGHT happen if you long rest is completely broken when you realize they won't, such as the hyenas being pregnant with gnolls for several days or karlach being wounded and a few meters aways from her hunters for several long rests, just standing there.[/spoiler]

While I'd love to go on about every inconvenience, some minor and some major, steam has a text limit, so I'll drop here.

So, in summary, the roleplaying game has too many events and scenarios which absolutely break the roleplaying aspect of it, for too many times has my immersion been broken, and basically I can't properly play as an evil character because the game isn't built with that in mind, it's built with you being a helpful hand. I won't recommend you play it if you're as picky as I am. But otherwise it's an okay game to turn your brain off to.
Really good game i thought it wasnt for me but its one of the best very fun.
I wasn't a fan of the game. But the playstyle and graphics were great.

hmmm.... this is a great rpg. ive enjoyed a few builds on it and i highly encourage people to buy this game. I give it a mighty 8-10.  beautiful graphics, precise gameplay and unforgetable characters.

The graphics are amazing, the story is amazing, the gameplay is amazing, and it keeps me coming back for more. I love this game so much, I highly highly recommend this game if you are a fan of turn based games! Highly addictive, and loads of fun! Getting to choose your decisions, create your character to your liking, mod the game, and so much more. It is definitely a game you will come back often to play.

I woke up after 50 hours jumping like a frog on some platforms like a turn-based Mario.

There are no tactics. There's a mnemonization of spells, items, and stats. Instead of thinking about which hill to place your shooter on, you wonder how the Magic Brooch of Ass you're wearing affects your rolls against the Sudden ♥♥♥♥ Spell.

They advertise that you can fight with the environment. Okay, you set something on fire, the enemy burns. It took a staggering 5% of their HP. So, back to the Sudden ♥♥♥♥ Spell stats. The only effective way to play the environment is to push them into a cliff, but the developers designed a loot loss to prevent anyone from playing like that. But if they stuck to "realism," the loot from a burned fireball victim would be unusable too.

The characters are crap. Protagonists and antagonists. Most attack both melee and ranged at the same time, making tactics pointless. They're jumping around like monkeys (a turn-based Mario), making it impossible to create any sort of structured gameplay. Battles are chaotic in the worst sense of the word.

The monkeys jump around like in a zoo, but at the same time, there are strict rules for blocking characters. You can't send your tank to the front because your team is blocking it. And it's set up the way it is because scripted dialogue set you up at the beginning of the battle. So, good luck with a tank at the back. This happens many times; you'd have to trigger conversations with the tank to keep it in the front. But why would you choose someone else as your main character if a tank with low charisma is supposed to talk?

Therefore, to do ANYTHING in this game, you need to have online help open because nothing is intuitive. There are difficult games, very difficult games, but intuitive. Where losing is fun. Here, you have great sessions with Gemini and discussions on how to prevent Gale from dying in the first turn. In BG3, losing is frustrating because you have no control over your initial setup, every enemy has a ranged attack, and everyone can jump like a monkey. It ends with you being surrounded and pelting your team with everything they can: arrows, spells, bottles of various substances.

That's what the Viet Cong did to every commando unit they spotted in the jungle in Vietnam. But those commandos were hiding, and my team of clowns has to engage in open battle. Often after swaggering dialogues.

As for the lesser sins, the inventory menu is clunky. Like something from 2000.
The camping system is tedious. Halfway through a dungeon, you have to return to camp because your spells and special moves have run out. Furthermore, camping is clearly at odds with the main plot, which counts down to a specific event in single days. Your camping days are unrelated to this; the dialogues constantly state you have a few days left until the event, but you've already camped a dozen times.

If you grit your teeth and endure what I've described, the game is beautiful. Maybe you're into D&D stats, then this is the game for you. But I had to tell the truth about the gameplay because it was touted as a game with good tactical combat, which it isn't.
There is a certain game called Skyrim that sucked hours of my time. Booted the game on a random morning, suddenly its night. BG3 is the only other game that did this. 5/5

An incredible game that make you feel like home. The characters and quests are beautifully written, also the locations make you stop to take a closer look. Great work from thousands of people. Thank you for this adventure
Great game. Didn't think I'd like it, ended up loving it. Top 5 game ever for me I think. The character writing and stories are second to none.

literally one of,if not, the best game ive ever played, if you like rpg's do yourself a favour and play this, thank me later, only negative things ive read about it is turned based combat bad or boring or that the game has lgbt stuff on it which i honestly coulnt care less since the game is so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ good
also if shadowheart was real i would 1000% simp for her
Incredible game. Highly recommend to anyone that enjoys engaging with a narrative and connecting with its characters. do NOT spoil any part of the story for yourself or others!!!
There is genuinely just so much to do in this game. It was a great experience. I never really had any issues personally with it, and the story was decent. I'd definitely recommend it.

i love this game so much, i would sell my first born child just to experience the joys of playing this game for the first time again. i cannot stress enough the importance of playing this game at least once in your life to feel your soul ascend to a higher plane of existence, the very essence of your being will be altered in a way you never thought possible from interacting with pixels on a screen. i am so completely normal about this game in the most abnormal of ways, i have taken it upon myself to immortalize it upon my very flesh with ink. if you will do anything for yourself, purchase baldur's gate 3 and embark on a wonderful journey that you will never forget. and make all your friends play it too. and talk about it with everyone you meet. and be so chill about characters that aren't even real. ooooo you wanna buy bg3 so bad oooooooo
(seriously thank you Larian for giving me my favorite video game on the planet <3)
This game is PEAK (not to be confused with the popular, enjoyable friendslop game, PEAK).  Highly recommend.
Truly one of the greatest RPG's made to date and the closest thing to the tabletop in video game form that you will find anywhere! 10/10

Please play it. Even if you are not a fan of DND, as a fan of RPG games in general you need to play this game. Hours upon hours of content and so many different choices to make, you will be replaying this game many times over. Once you are done, then download mods and replay the game with a whole new set of classes, gameplay changes and other game altering mods that will keep the game fresh.

Every ounce of praise and hype this game has received over the past 3 years have been warranted. One of the greatest RPGs ever crafted. HOWEVER, if you're planning to start a group run, there are a few caveats to be aware of:

- Dialogue is not designed around a group of 2+ custom characters, resulting in some awkward immersion-shaking dialogue scenes and story moments

- As far as we could tell, there is no way in the base game to accommodate for NPC companions if you have a full set of 4 people, resulting in missing out on key side quests and necessary character inclusions for main quests (which will cause some of your companions to completely turn on you and leave in a few cases)

- Only 1 player can really participate in dialogue, with the others only able to give suggestions. Very hard to tell which lines of dialogue are shared between all characters and which ones are only recognized by NPCs on an individual level

Co-op could have been much better implemented, but I fully understand how difficult it probably was to even get it in its current state without compromising the single-player or putting in a few extra million dollars for tons of extra dialogue. Therefore I absolutely still give it a high rating. Just figured it would be best to warn those who are buying it solely for co-op of the compromises.

I genuinely adore this game, it is one of the best games I've ever played. The game mechanics are simple and easy to learn, the graphics are outstanding and the storyline is immersive and highly interactive, every decision you make matters! One of the best aspect for me is the turn based fighting and as someone with a very slow reaction speed, it gives me time to really think through my actions and strategize rather than key smashing till my opponent's dead. The incorporation of D&D mechanics is also really fun because sometimes no amount of strategizing can hold up against the dice rolls. Also be prepared to spend plenty of time in this game. 132 hours and I'm still in Act 1 with 0 regrets.

All in all, it is a brilliant fantasy RPG, I HIGHLY recommend!
The definitive D&D video game. Such a massive game with so much depth. Faithful to 5e without being afraid to change the system to better suit the medium of a game.

I enjoy a wide variety of videogames, but there's something about CRPGs that will always be special to me. I still remember unwrapping my copy of STAR WARS Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords, installing it on my dad's old Windows XP box computer (yes, the grey one with a monitor as big as your head), and being transported into a new world of stories and gameplay. It probably ran at a crisp 15-20 frames per second and crashed constantly, but I didn't care. I was immersed in my character, forced to make choices that would alter the fate of party members I had painstakingly nurtured relationships with. 

Much, much further down the line in life, I become engrossed in Divinity: Original Sin II, a Kickstarted game from a relatively small indie studio. It consumed my every waking thought until the credits finally rolled. I of course enjoy many other CRPGs, but something about DOS 2 really gave me a taste of the obsession and immersion I had felt when I was younger. However, I wasn't sure I would experience that same feeling again any time soon.

When Baldur's Gate 3 launched out of early access and into full release, I took off a whole week and a half from my job to stay at home and experience it uninterrupted. It was the best PTO I've ever spent. I remember listening to Alfira's song in the Emerald Grove for the first time, a moment I could have easily missed depending on my dialogue options, and I recall being struck by how truly special this game was. I can't fault any other studios for not having the same level of production value as Larian, but the studio has a unique talent for writing character that really make you care so much about them, feel connected to them, relate to their struggles and empathize with them, motion captured cutscenes or no. 

After 120 hours of gameplay, I watched the credits roll. Unlike many other I've seen online, I couldn't bring myself to dive right back into the game. I needed a break. However, I have returned several times and completed many other playthroughs (though I still have not completed a Dark Urge playthrough, something I am currently working on). In fact, I completed Honor mode in 2024, and am only a handful of achievements away from 100%, something I'd never set out to do for any videogame I have played before. 

I also have to praise Larian for all the years they spent improving and expanding the game from 2023-2025 (for free, I might add).

Baldur's Gate 3 became a gateway for me: I found myself engrossed in D&D actual plays, learning tabletop systems, and even running some games of my own. Honestly, in that way, you could say this game changed my life, as TTRPGs and collaborative storytelling has become a large part of my life. 

I know this is a very long, rambling, poorly formatted review from off the top of my head, but if I had to sum up: The game is good, I think everyone should try it at least once.

And Thank You Larian, for creating a game that made me feel so in touch with my inner child again, and opening a new doorway for so many new fans to the genre.

First time playing an RPG with turn-based combat, prefer action based RPG.  I managed to play 30 mins of the game on a friends PC and really enjoyed it.  I love the interaction between characters and so far the story.  The turn based actions actually seem more relaxing and although I've had to play some battles a few times due to learning :) I did not get frustrated and just approach the battle differently.  Would highly recommend and well worth the asking price.  Will look forward to playing their other games in the future.
One of those games you can play over and over and get lost in for hours, or days, at a time

The First D&D Game i started in my life i enjoyed every second of it , when i bought the game i didn't 
understand of concept of the game until 1 month ago my friends got to play with me , i left that game because i couldn't use their choice but when i played this game solo i enjoyed every moment of this game , simply its you who creating your own story , i hardly recommend play your first playthrough solo then play with your friend , after the metal gear games this game lets me to feel i am playing a wonderful game its not just a game its a whole virtual adventure in a great world
The only thing I dislike about this game is how addicting it is. Please, BG3, I have a family I need to get to. I have work that needs to be done. Please free me of your addictive grasps

9.5/10. This game got me through the roughest period of my life (together with Elden Ring). There are no other games that have sucked me into a world and feeling like this game does. I don't want to write a long review, simply because I'm aware my writing isn't good enough for that, so all I'll say is that this game is 1 in a million.

Only reason I'm not giving it a 10/10 is because it's quite long, act 3 is also crazy big, and because of that I struggle to pick it back up in my busier than ever life.

This game has genuinely become my favourite game so quickly, so much so that when I finally got my own pc (I was borrowing my partners before) this was the first game I purchased.

It is incredibly story-rich and has so many different choices, approaches etc. that is allows for so much replayability. You can get incredibly creative in the way you approach different interactions, quests and so on. Furthermore the characters are so well written, they all have inticing backstories and unique personalities, and they make it so easy to get emotionally invested. It is the first videogame that has genuinely made me cry. As someone who's a massive DnD nerd, I was also especially impressed with how well they translated so many elements of DnD into a videogame format. 

I really couldn't reccommend it enough. Whether you're the type of player who just wants to experience a great story and have fun romancing a bunch of interesting characters, or you're into DnD and want a videogame that embodies that, or you're someone who's super into min-maxxing a character and finding new fun ways to deal with combat scenarios, or you just want to gather a group of friends and ♥♥♥♥ around in a big open world full of adventure, BG3 will have something for you.

It can make you laugh. It can make you cry. It can make your real world disappear as you explore the wonderful world of Faerûn.

The quality and attention to details in this game is stunning. You are automatically immersed into this world because of rich characters with full voice acting and a world with tons of lore. The combat system feels very smooth and allows you to do (almost) anything you have in mind.

Allowing for types of spells that have often been left out in other games in this genre, like Frineds, Detect Thoughts and Speak with Dead makes a spellcaster usable for other things than blasting your enemies. Most D&D players know that Guidance is the best spell in 5th edition. Larian knew that as well <3.

There are so many good things to say about this game but I will stop myself here and just say one more thing. Play this game if you like the genre, it will not disappoint.

As a RPG fanatic this game is an absolute masterpiece, the amount of player freedom is outstanding. The integrated modding support makes this an absolute 10/10

p.s Laezel is Baezel
Amazing game. Very complex and difficult to figure out. I did much better on a second playthrough. Highly recommend.

FINALLY a game with sex! I've been disappointed many a time by the lack of sex in many of my favorite games, such as Minecraft, Fortnite and especially Roblox. I decided to switch it up and play a different kind of game, and oh boy did it pay off. Shadowheart and I have made love a copious amount of times and will continue for the for see able future. Now my only problem is the lack of blumkins and Alabama hot pockets but ig the gameplay makes up for it or something. 8.93472/10

I bought this game when it came out but I only managed to finish it now. Mostly because after finishing each act my brain gets fooled into thinking that the game is over due to each act feeling like a complete game. I can comfortably say that this is definitely the game that I love the most and my #1 game of all time.

When I first heard about Baldur's Gate 3, it was while I was halfway through playing Divinity Original Sin 2 in 2023. Some friends and I had been going through DoS2 separately and they were expressing great excitement towards the game and it's imminent release. Of course I'd never heard about it but upon learning it was being created by the same developers responsible for the game I was currently enjoying (and the front runner for my favorite gaming experience so far that year), I looked it up and the rest is history.

I just finished my second full play through of the game, this time as a co-op play through with two friends. We started in April of 2025 and finished Mid-May 2026. What a journey it was. It honestly feels bittersweet that I get my Monday evenings back now because it means I've finished the grand adventure that is Baldur's Gate 3. And this time, likely for a long long time. 

There are games with better graphics, better animations, combat, mechanics, and possibly even better story. But the journey one goes on from waking up in the Nautiloid ship to finally arriving in Baldur's Gate proper is truly sublime. Larian crafted a masterpiece generational game that will be talked about like all the greats that came before it. Play it. Experience it. Enjoy it.

Obligatory "this game is so boring" comment despite having 1000+ hours. 

Anyway, this game is genuinely really well made. There are some downsides such as obviously cut content especially when it comes to Act 3/the end of the game and some of the companions (Wyll and Karlach especially), but overall the game is very enjoyable. There is a ton of content to explore, and I've found new stuff in every playthrough (and I thought I was thorough in my first 3 playthroughs lol). 

The built in mod manager makes modding the game incredibly easy even for technological nincompoops like me. I'll also admit that I'm also not usually the smartest or most motivated when it comes to building characters in games like this, but it's relatively easy to understand in BG3 (though I can't be sure if that's due to the DND building or not as I haven't had the change to play DND irl).

I've been playing this game since it came out, and I've had about 5 full playthroughs (you could obviously have far more than me in 1000+ hours, but I love Acts 1 & 2, so I always restart to replay them). If I had to explain why I don't like Act 3 as much without spoilers, I'd say it's because it's so overwhelming. There's not any sort of "recommended" order to do things in (which is understandable because it's supposed to be up to you), but I found Acts 1 & 2 much easier to follow without feeling like I had to do 10 things at once.

The companions and NPCs are well made characters for the most part (see my previous comment on cut content), and I love the reoccurring NPCs and their storylines. I think a lot of passion and community requests were put into many of the companions, but there is definitely a large gap between the content quantity of (for example) Shadowheart and Karlach or Astarion and Wyll. I am NOT saying it's bad that certain companions have a lot of content, and I adore all of the companions, but I just wish there were more to some of their personal stories and their romances. I will say that I highly enjoy the storylines of even the companions without as much content - just as much as I enjoy the longer content-having ones. However, there will always be a bit of a hole in their stories.

I've written quite a bit, and while I could talk about this game for ages, I'll stop here! Overall I'd say this is a good 8.5/10 game taking everything into account.
If you decide to play, have so much fun! It's really worth it! :)
It's just a D&D campaign, with plenty of freedom and secrets
I have managed to recreate my first-ever D&D character from when I was about 11 years old. A Martial Arts Dwarf.  Folks its fun its well made and the turn based action is fun.
Played since early access 2020 when nobody even gave a f about it. Wild. Would recommend but I wish I could unalive more NPCs

Possibly the best Intro to DnD RPG you will ever play. if you want a more "accurate to tabletop" try Divinity original sin 2 (or 3 when it eventually releases) Its made by the same company but both games are INCREDIBLE. Personally i have put Way more hours into baldur's gate 3 because its far more enjoyable to scratch that dnd itch in video game form. 

BG3 has amazing mod support so essentially infinite replayability - theres an ingame mod manager that is entirely click and play, super easy, sometimes a bit buggy but thats modding for you And if you want just an endless pool of mods you can use nexus and other 3rd party mod sites / programs. 

all in all its definitely worth the money, you will lose days playing this game (in a good way) 

10/10

The scale of this game and the amount of detail is beyond impressive. Because of the large variety of companions available, you can succeed with a variety of playstyles. I'd highly recommend this game. That being said, I WANT A HAPPIER ENDING FOR KARLACH ! :)

I looooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooove this game.

I was looking for a game with witches/sorcerers and came across this. I was a bit hesitant when I saw it was turn-based, but I decided to give it a shot since it’s so popular.

Now I’m emotionally attached. I started my second run immediately after finishing the first one. I cried during the epilogue and I’m writing this review during work hours (yes, I work on Saturdays).

I nearly didn’t play Baldur’s Gate 3.

Not because I don’t like RPGs - quite the opposite. Some of my favourite games of all time are RPGs. The reason I stayed away was because I bounced hard off Divinity: Original Sin 2. I know it’s a beloved game, but I found the combat slow, overwhelming and, if I’m honest, a bit boring. Every time someone recommended BG3, all I could think was:

“Isn’t that just Divinity combat again?”

I’m very glad I was wrong.

Baldur’s Gate 3 is quite possibly the best RPG I’ve ever played.

What sets it apart isn’t the graphics, the voice acting or the production value (all of which are exceptional). It’s the sheer amount of player agency. Ten different people can play this game and end up with ten genuinely different stories. Characters can live, die, betray you, be saved, be ignored or never even be met. Entire questlines can play out completely differently depending on your decisions.

My own playthrough ended with dead companions, unlikely alliances, a trip to Hell to rob a devil, and decisions I never would have predicted when I first woke up on a beach with a tadpole in my eye.

And here’s the thing I’d say to anyone who is on the fence because the game looks intimidating:

Give it a chance.

If character creation, classes, feats, spells and level-up screens look overwhelming, use a guide. Watch a video. Ask a friend. Use AI. Seriously. There is absolutely no shame in getting help understanding the systems. I did, and I’m convinced I enjoyed the game far more because of it.

Don’t let the complexity put you off experiencing one of the greatest RPGs ever made.

I came for the story.

I stayed for over 100 hours.

And when the credits rolled, I immediately started wondering what would have happened if I’d made different choices.

That’s probably the highest compliment I can give it.

It’s no surprise that this game has won so many awards. I want to thank the developers for putting their soul into this project.

What I liked: the combat system, build variety, voice acting, graphics, environmental interactions, visual style, and memorable characters.

What I didn't like: the plot is dragged out and very simple, especially once the initial intrigue is over. I also disliked that there is no option to reject all factions and go against everyone.
The third act feels too long and overloaded, especially if you play the game without taking breaks. If you carefully explored everything earlier, by the time you reach Act 3, you just don't have the energy to push forward; it simply becomes boring.
Another issue is that even on the highest difficulty, the game becomes very easy at high levels. Neither boss fights nor crowds of hostile NPCs pose any challenge.
For me, the ideal ending was the end of Act 2. I think the game could have ended right there. After Act 2, I spent most of my time just testing different builds and combat tactics. Having tried everything, I stopped playing without ever finishing Act 3.

I hope the plot in Larian’s next game will be deep and unpredictable enough to keep players engaged until the end without getting boring. In any case, it's a great game, and I recommend everyone try it at least once.

This is my first turn based game, I'm enjoying it thus far. It's also my first game in the DnD universe. I don't have really any complaints, however I don't really understand the "Game of the Year" love that this got.

I accidentally got into a massive fight with like 50 rats. My character died and while the rest of my party was on the verge of death I made one of my characters use a spell that causes a corpse to explode. I exploded myself to kill all the rats and then revived my character... 10/10 game would recommend

After over 328 hours and numerous playthroughs on this laptop, the game is unplayable. Fresh install of the game, fresh install on Windows, fresh drivers. Getting screen locked on the black loading screen before the Larian logo loads., where you have to manually shut off your PC and restart it . Worked fo an evening, then nothing, and what I diud before didnt work. Numerous people are having issues with this and the numbers are increasing. I dont have the time or the patience to fight with this anymore after fighting with it for 3 days.
Fun game. Gives you the D&D feels. Great for post surgery recovery gaming, you can stand up and walk away without drooling GenZer's screaming at you over discord for taking too long.
Great game, enjoyed every bit of it. The characters, the story and gameplay are all top tier. Never played D&D before, might look into it now!

Incredible, I am finally trying the honor mode and it made me fall back in love with the game, and also prevents me from save-scumming, making rolls feel important again. I am loving it way too much but if I lose the run it will destroy me. Also I felt inclined to make a review at 666 hours, hoping it will bless my run when I face the best song in the game down the line. G'day!
Perfect game for 50+ yr old DND dork dads who sake it at gaming and reaction times. Love this game. Be forewarned, surprisingly kinky.

90%
+ Fantastic graphics
+ Smooth, fluid turn-based action
+ Lots of choices matter
+ Strong story
- Ending is underwhelming
- Third act is weak
- Some quests have poor direction

discovering new scenes I missed every single play session! just by going in a slightly different order, different race, class, morality, and just through rng on rolls and skills checks! I don't think I'll ever see absolutely everything this game has to offer!

Spent and spending a ridiculous amount of time in this game. Such a beautiful game and a truly immersive experience. Made with such love toward imagination and the perfect amount of challenge. Truly believe there will be nothing quite like it ever again.

As a lifelong D&D fan and someone who’s been gaming for decades, Baldur’s Gate 3 hits a level of quality and passion that’s honestly rare in modern games. This is the closest any game has come to capturing the feeling of sitting around a table with friends, rolling dice, and watching a story unfold in ways you never expected.

I’ve already put over 400 hours into BG3, and I can easily see myself sinking another 400 without hesitation. Every playthrough feels fresh thanks to the incredible writing, branching choices, and the sheer amount of detail packed into every corner of the world.

The graphics are stunning, the sound design is rich and immersive, and the music is absolutely unforgettable. But what really stands out is the obvious love and effort Larian poured into this game. It’s polished, it’s ambitious, and it respects the player’s time and imagination in a way few games do anymore.

I genuinely wish Larian were making Baldur’s Gate 4, because they’ve proven they understand this universe and its spirit better than anyone. BG3 isn’t just a great game, it’s a masterpiece, and one I’ll be coming back to for years.
I have never so quickly gotten attached to a game. These guys are in my brain forever.
This game is beyond good. one of the few games I truly love and spent many hours in and will contiue too.

This game is so much fun and has great re-playability. Each play through is different and there are so many options. You also discover new things with each play through. I love how the choices affect things in the game and the ability to mod adds even more content.
really cool strategy and it has so unfamiliar mechanics for me
Deep story, compelling characters and challenging gameplay.
Amazing game. You get to be creative in how you approach issues. This is the closest to actually playing D&D I've gotten on a computer.
Fantastic game! I love the story, the characters, and the huge capacity for creative freedom. I'm on my fourth full playthrough and I'm still discovering things I haven't seen in my other games.

Good game, was actually able to complete a full campaign in multiplayer after restarting a few times to create new characters trying new classes. 
It's a common theme apparently to keep repeating Act1 with new player made characters, just because. 

In multiplayer however, you do miss out on unique interactions from npc character compositions.

It's hard to get bored of due to the vast amount of things and secrets you can see and do. There are so many detail you'll miss and you can play it over and over again using different races or choosing different options to get a different run/dialogue options. Overall, I love this game.
Wonderful Story great game play, Fun to play with friends lol
Literally the best game of all time. Thank you to my friend for giving me this game it’s genuinely changed my life
Pretty great, freedom is what makes games great. That is where baldur's gate 3 truly shines.
Great game. Prepare for a time sink if you want to complete everything.
In depth gameplay and masterful storytelling. I've put over 700 hours in this game and still find enjoyment playing.

Incredible storytelling (really, perhaps one of the best rpgs I've played), mechanics (you can get extremely creative all things considered), and community. Baldur's Gate 3 is probably the closest you'll get to D&D without actually playing D&D -- including the inability to get your friends together consistently enough to finish a campaign. If I really had to nitpick, the character creator is a bit lackluster for a D&D game, lacking detailed face customization and real variance in body types. There are plenty of mods to fix that though, which are integrated, as well! All in all, heavily recommend. Sadly, however, there is no fishing. Perhaps the next larian game will ease my fish-lacking woes.

always takes me a bit to adjust to turn based games, no aspect of covering fire, so flushing tactics look a lil different. barbarian can just take the chests until you get a rouge to pick them is kinda fun

Incredible. Rich story and deep gameplay both packed with surprises - delightful and distressing - that will make "I can't believe..." a party catchphrase.

A word of caution: If you're a compulsive over-optimizer, you [i]must not[/i] play this game unless you're with friends who have no patience for your ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. God forbid you find yourself alongside another compulsive over-optimizer - I fear to imagine the ensuing menu-wiki purgatory. Someone save us from ourselves.

Fun by yourself, although it might be a lot for new players having to manage more than one character but it's a good way to learn different classes at once. Exceptionally more fun with friends; mods allow you to fine-tune your experience. Monk go brrrt.
It's a pretty good game. Don't look at my hours just trust. I can, and will play more.

I love this game to death!!!
You can explore hours and still be suprised; recommended for anyone willing to try a DND style game

Level 6 brain rot. Still not even close to bored after almost 2k hours of gameplay. Gorgeous graphics. Gorgeous storyline/s. Gorgeous music (deadass has become my favourite game OST). Gameplay is easy to learn even for those unfamiliar with D&D while still presenting a challenge. Difficulty and other settings are very modular, easy to personalise for accessibility or just individual preference. Its GotY awards are well deserved.

WOW, what a game! It took alot for me to put this one down. The characters are well written, the narrative really good! The mechanics and overall application of Dnd are done really well. The presentation is this games claim to fame though. The cinematic presentation BG3 has is unrivaled and pushes the story beats to another level. Just wow. Overall an amazing experieince! I can't wait to see what Larian does next. Seeing their evolution from the Divinity: Origional Sin games to this has been a real treat. <3

I'm honestly surprised this game garnered so much mainstream attention. 

It is a solid CPRG, but it is also flush with bugs, missing obvious QoL improvements, and has a rather steep learning curve.

It's a good CRPG with notable moments, but far from best-in-class. 

Playing this just made me want to go back to DAO.

Frankly one of the best games of all time. Genre Defining. The ambition and scope of this game is staggering. Larian Studio's cemented themselves as one of the best developers OAT. Also, you get to ♥♥♥♥ Gale

Easily one of my top 10 most enjoyed games. I got 160 hrs out of just my first full playthrough and there are still more branches of this story to explore. gameplay is just like playing D&D. The stories and relationships that can be had are quite extensive.
I want everyone in the world to play BG3 at least once. Favorite game of all time. I love it so much I have a potion of minor healing tattooed on my arm lol. Please play it.
Divinity 2 mixed with DnD, amazing with and without friends. There are bugs, but they can be resolved with a restart or something even simpler. Amazing story and enjoyable combat.

This is the most satisfying RPG I have ever played. Despite having essentially 1 storyline, the way all of the choices you make impact how the story unfolds and ultimately resolves is executed to perfection.

Top notch game. Cant wait to start another play through and choose completely different styles of combat and character stories.
Ridiculously good game!!!
I really like this game not only for the graphic and the storyline but also because you can play this game so many different ways and get different scenarios. Plus it helps when you have DnD itch.
Several years late to review this, but Baldur's Gate 3 is a game rich in storytelling with wonderful mechanics and maximum replayability.

I have played hundred of hours of Baldur's Gate 3 on console, I own it on literally every available platform. I love this game so much. To me, this game feels like a modern Skyrim, a genuine masterpiece of replay value with a large emphasis on the value.

A famous astrophysicist said “The Universe is under no obligation to make sense to you.”
Neither does Larian's way of making games. What Matters is that they are both wonderful.
long and expansive game with lots of customization and replayablilty, interesting and deep characters. modding to spice up any playthrough

Absolute cinema, I made sure to grind out every achievement.

Barely played with mods cuz the base game stands amazingly by itself. You can still find new things to do after hundreds of hours.

Sometimes the combat can be a bit questionable but on the whole its good. The character interactions and story is really where this game shines.
I don't want to write long feedbacks because if you read the other people's comments, it says everything I have to say. I love this game. It's my number 1 favourite.

I've been playing this game off and on for a couple years and it is worth the money due to excellent story telling, gameplay, detail in everything, choose your own adventure-ish gameplay and plenty more. I can set this game down and come back to it whenever. I can pause it when I have to go to work then jump right back in the game where I left off.
Great game. Runs very well on my 6 year old gaming rig at full graphics. Very immersive.
